story.iWant: a universal data structure that can represent all photo editing parameters from any supported format
story.soThat: I can convert between formats without N² conversion functions and without data loss
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Define UniversalRecipe core struct (AC: #1) - Create internal/models/recipe.go - Define UniversalRecipe struct with all photo editing parameters - Add JSON and XML struct tags for serialization - Add field documentation comments
- Task 2: Define nested data structures (AC: #2) - Create ColorAdjustment struct (hue, saturation, luminance int) - Create ToneCurvePoint struct (input, output float64) - Create SplitToning struct (highlightHue, highlightSaturation, shadowHue, shadowSaturation) - Create CameraProfile struct (camera calibration settings)
- Task 3: Implement validation functions (AC: #3) - Create internal/models/validation.go - Implement ValidateExposure(value float64) error - Implement ValidatePercentage(value int) error for -100 to +100 range - Implement ValidateHSL(hue, sat, lum int) error - Implement ValidateNP3Ranges for NP3-specific parameters - Add Validate() method to UniversalRecipe
- Task 4: Implement Builder pattern (AC: #5) - Create internal/models/builder.go - Define RecipeBuilder struct - Implement NewRecipeBuilder() constructor - Implement setter methods with validation (WithExposure, WithContrast, etc.) - Implement Build() method that validates and returns UniversalRecipe - Add error handling for invalid parameter combinations
- Task 5: Write unit tests (AC: #6) - Create internal/models/recipe_test.go - Test struct serialization to JSON - Test struct serialization to XML - Test round-trip JSON encoding/decoding - Test round-trip XML encoding/decoding - Test validation functions with valid and invalid inputs - Test builder pattern with various parameter combinations - Test edge cases (min/max values, zero values, nil handling) - Verify 95%+ test coverage with go test -cover

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. UniversalRecipe Core Struct: Define Go struct with all common photo editing parameters - Basic adjustments: exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows, whites, blacks - Color adjustments: temperature, tint, vibrance, saturation - HSL adjustments: 8 colors (red, orange, yellow, green, aqua, blue, purple, magenta) × 3 properties (hue, saturation, luminance) - Tone curve: parametric curve points for highlights, lights, darks, shadows - Split toning: highlight and shadow color tinting - Sharpening, clarity, dehaze, vignette parameters
2. Nested Structs: Define supporting data structures - ColorAdjustment: HSL triplet (hue, saturation, luminance) - ToneCurvePoint: Point on tone curve (input, output values) - SplitToning: Highlight/shadow hue and saturation - CameraProfile: Camera calibration settings
3. Validation Functions: Implement range validation for all parameters - Exposure: -5.0 to +5.0 - Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks: -100 to +100 - Saturation, Vibrance: -100 to +100 - HSL values: hue (-180 to +180), saturation/luminance (-100 to +100) - NP3 ranges: sharpening (0-9), contrast (-3 to +3), brightness (-1 to +1), saturation (-3 to +3), hue (-9 to +9)
4. Serialization Support: Add struct tags for JSON and XML marshaling - JSON tags for file export and debugging - XML tags for XMP format generation - Omitempty tags for optional fields
5. Builder Pattern: Implement builder for safe UniversalRecipe construction - Required field validation - Default value initialization - Method chaining for fluent API - Build() method that validates and returns immutable instance
6. Test Coverage: Achieve 95%+ test coverage - Unit tests for all validation functions - Round-trip JSON serialization tests - Round-trip XML serialization tests - Edge case tests (min/max values, invalid ranges)

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Central UniversalRecipe data structure for format-agnostic conversion using hub-and-spoke pattern. Single data structure captures superset of 50+ parameters from all formats with graceful handling of format-specific features.

artifact.title: Architecture Documentation
artifact.snippet: Reduces converter complexity from O(N²) to O(N). UniversalRecipe serves as intermediate representation with centralized validation and normalization. Adding 4th format requires 2 functions (not 6 converters).

artifact.title: Migration Guide
artifact.section: Phase 1: Core Library - Week 1: Data Structures
artifact.snippet: Define UniversalRecipe struct with JSON/XML tags, nested structs (ColorAdjustment, ToneCurvePoint, CameraProfile), validation functions, builder pattern. Checkpoint 1.1: 95%+ test coverage required.

artifact.title: Technology Stack
artifact.snippet: Hub-and-spoke conversion architecture pattern where all formats convert to/from central UniversalRecipe structure (NP3 → UniversalRecipe → XMP).

artifact.reason: Python @dataclass with all field names, types, and default values. Provides reference for parameter names and ranges to use in Go implementation.
Nested struct for HSL triplet (hue, saturation, luminance) with documented ranges.
Nested struct for tone curve points with input/output values.
Nested struct for camera calibration settings (red/green/blue hue and saturation).

package.name: encoding/json
package.purpose: JSON marshaling for file export and debugging

package.name: encoding/xml
package.purpose: XML marshaling for XMP format generation

story-context.constraints:
Architecture Pattern - Hub-and-Spoke: - UniversalRecipe serves as central hub for all format conversions - All parsers convert: Format → UniversalRecipe - All generators convert: UniversalRecipe → Format - Eliminates N² conversion complexity (only need N parsers + N generators)
Immutability Design: - Once constructed via Builder, UniversalRecipe should be immutable - Use Builder pattern to construct, then return read-only struct - Prevents mutation bugs during multi-step conversions - Consider using unexported fields with exported getter methods
Go Version Requirements: - Use Go 1.21+ features (built-in min/max functions, modern type inference) - Enhanced error handling patterns
Dependency Constraints: - Standard library only for core data structures - No external dependencies - Use encoding/json for JSON marshaling - Use encoding/xml for XML marshaling
Project Structure: - internal/models/recipe.go - UniversalRecipe and nested structs - internal/models/validation.go - Validation functions - internal/models/builder.go - Builder pattern implementation - internal/models/recipe_test.go - Unit tests
story-context.interfaces:
No existing interfaces - this is the foundational data structure.
Future interfaces that will consume UniversalRecipe: - Format parsers (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate) will create UniversalRecipe instances - Format generators will accept UniversalRecipe and produce format-specific output

tests.standards: Use Go's standard testing package with table-driven tests. Place tests in same package (internal/models) for white-box testing. Target 95%+ code coverage per migration guide requirements. Run tests with: go test -v -cover ./internal/models/... Verify coverage meets checkpoint criteria before PR.
tests.locations: - internal/models/recipe_test.go (all data structure and validation tests) - Future: Use 1,501 sample files from examples/ directory for integration testing
tests.ideas:
AC #1 - UniversalRecipe Core Struct: - Test struct field initialization with zero values - Test JSON marshaling includes all fields with correct tags - Test XML marshaling includes all fields with correct tags - Test omitempty tags exclude zero values from serialization
AC #2 - Nested Structs: - Test ColorAdjustment creation and field access - Test ToneCurvePoint creation with valid input/output ranges - Test SplitToning struct field initialization - Test CameraProfile struct field initialization
AC #3 - Validation Functions: - Table-driven test for ValidateExposure: valid (-5.0 to +5.0), invalid (outside range) - Table-driven test for ValidatePercentage: valid (-100 to +100), invalid (outside range) - Table-driven test for ValidateHSL: valid hue (-180 to +180), saturation/luminance (-100 to +100) - Table-driven test for ValidateNP3Ranges: sharpening (0-9), contrast (-3 to +3), etc. - Test Validate() method returns error for invalid parameter combinations
AC #4 - Serialization Support: - Test round-trip JSON: UniversalRecipe → JSON → UniversalRecipe (equality check) - Test round-trip XML: UniversalRecipe → XML → UniversalRecipe (equality check) - Test omitempty excludes default values from output - Test field names match expected JSON/XML tag values
AC #5 - Builder Pattern: - Test NewRecipeBuilder() initializes with defaults - Test WithExposure validates and sets value correctly - Test WithContrast and other setters validate ranges - Test Build() succeeds with valid parameters - Test Build() fails with invalid parameter combinations - Test method chaining works (builder.WithExposure(2.0).WithContrast(50).Build())
AC #6 - Test Coverage: - Run go test -cover and verify ≥95% coverage - Test edge cases: min values, max values, zero values - Test nil handling for optional fields - Test error messages are clear and actionable
